document.title: LA32R 基础整数与基础特权指令语义（RTL 核对版）
document.source_pdf: 龙架构32位精简版开发手册.pdf
document.encoding_note: 本文件面向功能与语义核对。encoding 是可选扩展字段；由于转换稿附录 B 存在跨页错位，首版不转录二进制编码，译码常量应从原 PDF 复核。

schema.extension_rule: 新增指令集时向 instruction_sets 追加对象；优先复用 profiles 和 operand_forms，不足时新增具名定义。每个具体助记符使用独立 instruction 对象。

architectural_state.gr_zero: r0 reads as 0 and discards writes

execution_model.normal_next_pc: PC_old + 4 unless a taken control-flow instruction or exception supplies another PC
execution_model.source_before_destination: All architectural source values are sampled before destination writes of the same instruction.
execution_model.r0_rule: Writing r0 suppresses only the register-file write; all other effects and exceptions still occur.
execution_model.precise_exception_rule: On a synchronous instruction exception, normal destination and memory side effects are suppressed and the faulting PC is recorded by the exception mechanism.
execution_model.memory_exception_priority: For an aligned-required data access, ALE has priority over its TLB/page exception.
execution_model.tlb_exception_exclusivity: One memory access raises at most one TLB/page-related exception.

notation.PC_old: address of the current instruction before architectural PC update
notation.MEMn[a]: little-endian n-bit memory access at address a

plv0_instruction: IPE when current PLV is not 0
cacop: IPE for non-Hit operation outside PLV0
Hit operation may raise address-translation/TLB page exceptions
invtlb: IPE when current PLV is not 0

rtl_checks: Do not trap on signed overflow or carry-out.
Do not trap on signed overflow or borrow.
In LA32R the concatenation already produces exactly 32 bits.
The immediate is sign-extended even though the comparison is unsigned.

instructions.operation: No architectural state change other than normal PC advance.

rtl_checks: The low 32 bits are identical for signed and unsigned multiplication.

undefined_behavior: When divisor GR[rk] is zero, the result may be any value; no exception is raised.
When divisor GR[rk] is zero, the result may be any value; no exception is raised.

notes: For nonzero divisor, the remainder has the dividend sign and smaller absolute value than the divisor.
undefined_behavior: When divisor GR[rk] is zero, the result may be any value; no exception is raised.
When divisor GR[rk] is zero, the result may be any value; no exception is raised.

instructions.operation: if GR[rj] == GR[rd]: PC = PC_old + SEXT(offs16 << 2,32)
if GR[rj] != GR[rd]: PC = PC_old + SEXT(offs16 << 2,32)
if signed32(GR[rj]) < signed32(GR[rd]): PC = PC_old + SEXT(offs16 << 2,32)
if signed32(GR[rj]) >= signed32(GR[rd]): PC = PC_old + SEXT(offs16 << 2,32)
if unsigned32(GR[rj]) < unsigned32(GR[rd]): PC = PC_old + SEXT(offs16 << 2,32)
if unsigned32(GR[rj]) >= unsigned32(GR[rd]): PC = PC_old + SEXT(offs16 << 2,32)
base = GR[rj]; GR[rd] = PC_old + 4; PC = base + SEXT(offs16 << 2,32)
rtl_checks: Read GR[rj] before writeback when rd equals rj.
A misaligned target is observed as a later instruction-fetch ADEF.

instructions.operation: vaddr = GR[rj] + SEXT(si12,32); require vaddr[0]==0; GR[rd] = SEXT(MEM16[vaddr],32)
vaddr = GR[rj] + SEXT(si12,32); require vaddr[0]==0; GR[rd] = ZEXT(MEM16[vaddr],32)
vaddr = GR[rj] + SEXT(si12,32); require vaddr[1:0]==0; GR[rd] = MEM32[vaddr]
vaddr = GR[rj] + SEXT(si12,32); require vaddr[0]==0; MEM16[vaddr] = GR[rd][15:0]
vaddr = GR[rj] + SEXT(si12,32); require vaddr[1:0]==0; MEM32[vaddr] = GR[rd]
vaddr = GR[rj] + SEXT(si12,32); hint 0 requests load-prefetch to L1 DCache; hint 8 requests store-prefetch to L1 DCache
writes: implementation cache state when request is honored
rtl_checks: Undefined hints act as NOP.
Non-cached target acts as NOP.
Never raise MMU or address-related exceptions.

instructions.operation: vaddr = GR[rj] + SEXT(si14 << 2,32); GR[rd] = MEM32[vaddr]; reservation_address = vaddr; LLbit = 1
data = old(GR[rd]); vaddr = GR[rj] + SEXT(si14 << 2,32); if LLbit==1 and reservation remains valid then MEM32[vaddr]=data and success=1 else no store and success=0; GR[rd]=success
rtl_checks: Capture old GR[rd] before writing the success flag.
A coherent store to the reserved cache line by another agent clears LLbit.

instructions.operation: Apply the memory-ordering constraint selected by hint; hint 0 is a full load/store barrier
side_effects: For hint 0, all earlier loads/stores complete before the barrier completes; later loads/stores start only after it completes.
rtl_checks: Any hint without a dedicated implementation must behave as hint 0.

instructions.operation: Synchronize prior stores with subsequent instruction fetch; hint 0 is the mandatory form
rtl_checks: After IBAR 0, subsequent fetch must observe all stores before the barrier.

instructions.operation: Raise SYS synchronously; code is available to the exception handler
Raise BRK synchronously; code is available to the exception handler

notes: Separate high/low reads are not guaranteed to be one atomic 64-bit snapshot.
Appendix B labels the encoding RDCNTID.W, while the semantic section uses RDCNTID.
An undefined or unimplemented CSR reads as zero.

instructions.operation: old_csr = CSR[csr_num]; write software-writable CSR fields from old(GR[rd]); GR[rd] = old_csr
rtl_checks: Capture both old values before either architectural write.
Writes to read-only and reserved fields follow the CSR field access rules.
notes: For an undefined or unimplemented CSR, no CSR state changes and GR[rd] receives zero.

instructions.operation: old_csr = CSR[csr_num]; new_csr = (old_csr & ~GR[rj]) | (old(GR[rd]) & GR[rj]) for software-writable bits; GR[rd] = old_csr
rtl_checks: GR[rj] is the per-bit write mask.
CSR hazards are interlocked by hardware; software barriers are not required.
notes: For an undefined or unimplemented CSR, no CSR state changes and GR[rd] receives zero.

instructions.operation: VA = GR[rj] + SEXT(si12,32); code[2:0] selects cache; code[4:3] selects StoreTag, Index maintenance, Hit maintenance, or implementation-defined operation
side_effects: code[2:0]=2 selects L2 shared unified cache.
code[4:3]=0 performs direct-index StoreTag initialization and writes tag zero.
code[4:3]=1 performs direct-index invalidate or invalidate-and-writeback.
code[4:3]=2 performs hit-based invalidate or invalidate-and-writeback after address translation.
rtl_checks: Instruction-cache invalidation does not require data writeback.
Hit operations do not require natural address alignment.

instructions.operation: Search TLB using CSR.ASID.ASID and CSR.TLBEHI.VPPN; on hit set TLBIDX.Index=hit_index and TLBIDX.NE=0, otherwise set TLBIDX.NE=1
rtl_checks: TLB indices are numbered from zero in increasing row order.

instructions.operation: Read TLB[CSR.TLBIDX.Index]; for an enabled entry copy ASID, VPPN, PS and both subpage fields to related CSRs and set NE=0; for a disabled entry set NE=1 and clear ASID, TLBEHI, TLBELO0, TLBELO1 and PS
undefined_behavior: If CSR.TLBIDX.Index is outside the implemented TLB range, behavior is undefined.
rtl_checks: TLB entry enable E and subpage valid bits V0/V1 are different concepts.

instructions.operation: Build a TLB entry from CSR.ASID, TLBEHI, TLBELO0, TLBELO1 and TLBIDX.PS, then write TLB[TLBIDX.Index]; E = 1 when ESTAT.Ecode==0x3f, otherwise E = !TLBIDX.NE
rtl_checks: During TLB refill handling, force the entry enabled regardless of TLBIDX.NE.
Entry G is one only when both TLBELO0.G and TLBELO1.G are one.

instructions.operation: Build the same entry as TLBWR but write a hardware-randomly selected TLB slot; E = 1 when ESTAT.Ecode==0x3f, otherwise E = !TLBIDX.NE
rtl_checks: The replacement index is selected by hardware, not CSR.TLBIDX.Index.
During TLB refill handling, force the entry enabled.

instructions.operation: Invalidate every TLB entry satisfying the predicate selected by op, using ASID=GR[rj][9:0] and VA=GR[rk] when required
side_effects: op 0x4: invalidate G=0 entries whose ASID equals GR[rj][9:0].
op 0x5: invalidate G=0 entries whose ASID and VA match.
op 0x6: invalidate entries whose VA matches and for which G=1 or ASID matches.
rtl_checks: For op 0x6 use (G || ASID_match) && VA_match.
Use r0 for an unused ASID or VA operand.

instructions.operation: CRMD.PLV=PRMD.PPLV; CRMD.IE=PRMD.PIE; if ESTAT.Ecode==0x3f then CRMD.DA=0 and CRMD.PG=1; PC=ERA; if LLBCTL.KLO!=1 then LLbit=0; clear LLBCTL.KLO after its one-shot effect
rtl_checks: TLB-refill return restores mapped mode with DA=0 and PG=1.
KLO preserves LLbit for one ERTN only and then auto-clears.

instructions.operation: Complete the instruction, stop fetching, and wait for interrupt wakeup or reset; after interrupt wakeup the resume point is PC_old+4
writes: PC on wakeup or reset path
notes: The architectural function of level is not further defined by this manual.
